subject: GRB241016C: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

"At 16:19:50.85 UT on 16 October 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB241016C (trigger 750788395/241016680).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 108.68, Dec = -40.82 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 7h 14m, -40d 49'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 14.90 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 35 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241016680/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn241016680.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241016680/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn241016680.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241016680/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn241016680.gif"
